content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA, Output the sum of all elements in AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer NNN denoting the number of elements in AAA.
the second line contains NNN space-separated integers denoting elements of the array AAA.


Output Format
Output a single integer, sum of all the elements in the array AAA.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of elements in the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum of the N integers of that test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. The array [8,2,4,1,4] sums to 8+2+4+1+4 = 19.
Single test case, sum of [-1,0,1] is 0.

solution.approach: The required sum can be obtained by iterating over the array once and accumulating the values in a 64‑bit integer. Since each test case is independent, we reset the accumulator for every test case. This linear scan is optimal because every element must be read at least once. The algorithm runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int N; scanf("%d",&N); long long sum=0; for(int i=0;i<N;i++){int x; scanf("%d",&x); sum+=x;} printf("%lld\n",sum);} return 0; }
code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        N=int(next(it))
        total=0
        for _ in range(N):
            total+=int(next(it))
        out.append(str(total))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
